diff --git a/sphinx/util/inspect.py b/sphinx/util/inspect.py
index a415a7074..9af90ef27 100644
--- a/sphinx/util/inspect.py
+++ b/sphinx/util/inspect.py
@@ -432,6 +432,10 @@ def safe_getattr(obj: Any, name: str, *defargs: Any) -> Any:
 
 def object_description(object: Any) -> str:
     """A repr() implementation that returns text safe to use in reST context."""
+    if isenumattribute(object):
+        # For enum values, show the qualified name instead of the repr
+        # e.g., MyEnum.ValueA instead of <MyEnum.ValueA: 10>
+        return '%s.%s' % (object.__class__.__name__, object.name)
     if isinstance(object, dict):
         try:
             sorted_keys = sorted(object)
diff --git a/sphinx/util/typing.py b/sphinx/util/typing.py
index af6edcae0..792a76397 100644
--- a/sphinx/util/typing.py
+++ b/sphinx/util/typing.py
@@ -308,6 +308,14 @@ def stringify(annotation: Any) -> str:
     elif (getattr(annotation, '__module__', None) == 'builtins' and
           hasattr(annotation, '__qualname__')):
         return annotation.__qualname__
+    elif inspect.isenumclass(annotation):
+        # For enum classes, show the qualified name
+        # e.g., MyEnum instead of <MyEnum.ValueA: 1>
+        return annotation.__name__
+    elif inspect.isenumattribute(annotation):
+        # For enum values, show the qualified name
+        # e.g., ugly_enum.MyEnum instead of <ugly_enum.MyEnum: 1>
+        return '%s.%s' % (annotation.__class__.__name__, annotation.name)
     elif annotation is Ellipsis:
         return '...'
 
diff --git a/tests/test_util_inspect.py b/tests/test_util_inspect.py
index de4ad9236..4836f526e 100644
--- a/tests/test_util_inspect.py
+++ b/tests/test_util_inspect.py
@@ -10,6 +10,7 @@
 
 import ast
 import datetime
+import enum
 import functools
 import sys
 import types
@@ -516,6 +517,17 @@ def test_dict_customtype():
     assert "<CustomType(2)>: 2" in description
 
 
+def test_enum_attribute():
+    class MyEnum(enum.Enum):
+        ValueA = 10
+        ValueB = 20
+
+    # Test that enum values are displayed as Name.Value, not <Name.Value: value>
+    description = inspect.object_description(MyEnum.ValueA)
+    assert description == "MyEnum.ValueA"
+    description = inspect.object_description(MyEnum.ValueB)
+    assert description == "MyEnum.ValueB"
+
 def test_getslots():
     class Foo:
         pass
diff --git a/tests/test_util_typing.py b/tests/test_util_typing.py
index d3672f2cc..42b6caec8 100644
--- a/tests/test_util_typing.py
+++ b/tests/test_util_typing.py
@@ -8,6 +8,7 @@
     :license: BSD, see LICENSE for details.
 """
 
+import enum
 import sys
 from numbers import Integral
 from struct import Struct
@@ -258,3 +259,13 @@ def test_stringify_type_union_operator():
 
 def test_stringify_broken_type_hints():
     assert stringify(BrokenType) == 'tests.test_util_typing.BrokenType'
+
+
+def test_stringify_enum_values():
+    # Test that enum values are displayed as ClassName.Value, not <ClassName.Value: value>
+    class MyEnum(enum.Enum):
+        ValueA = 10
+        ValueB = 20
+    
+    assert stringify(MyEnum.ValueA) == 'MyEnum.ValueA'
+    assert stringify(MyEnum.ValueB) == 'MyEnum.ValueB'
